findings.evidence: PR lines 173-182 contain the `show` rewrite; Mathlib has `ModN` as `G ⧸ LinearMap.range ...` at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/FreeModule/ModN.lean:25-26`, while `AddSubgroup.index_eq_card` is an index-as-quotient-cardinality statement at `Mathlib/GroupTheory/Index.lean:348-349`.
findings.fix: Factor an explicit equivalence/cardinality lemma between `ModN (Additive G) 2` and the quotient by the corresponding `AddSubgroup`, then use `Nat.card_congr`/that lemma instead of a `show` defeq rewrite.
findings.issue: `card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_index_square` proves the cardinality-index bridge with `rw [show Nat.card (ElementaryTwoQuotient G) = ...toAddSubgroup.index from ...]`, relying on `ModN` unfolding to a `Submodule` quotient and `AddSubgroup.index` unfolding to quotient cardinality.

summary: One public bridge theorem still relies on undocumented definitional equality across quotient/index wrappers, making the proof brittle under Mathlib implementation changes.
